definition: a choice made after thinking about different options

sentences.text: She made an important decision.
It was a difficult decision to leave.
We need more information before making a decision.

definition: ready to be used or obtained

sentences.text: Is this product available today?
The manager is not available now.
Several options are available online.

definition: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.text: You can achieve your goals with practice.
She worked hard to achieve better results.
The team achieved its target.

definition: to need something for a particular purpose

sentences.text: This task will require more time.
The job requires strong communication skills.
Some repairs require professional help.

definition: to make something smaller or less

sentences.text: The company wants to reduce costs.
We should reduce plastic use.
This medicine may reduce the pain.

definition: to give someone something they need

sentences.text: The course will provide useful information.
The hotel provides free breakfast.
Please provide your contact details.

definition: to stay away from something or prevent it from happening

sentences.text: Try to avoid unnecessary mistakes.
He avoids driving during rush hour.
Regular checks help avoid problems.

definition: to mention an idea for someone to consider

sentences.text: I would suggest taking the earlier train.
She suggested a different approach.
Can you suggest a good restaurant?

definition: to succeed in doing something difficult

sentences.text: She managed to solve the problem alone.
Can you manage the project by yourself?
He managed to finish before the deadline.

definition: to be decided or influenced by something else

sentences.text: The result will depend on your effort.
Prices depend on the season.
You can depend on her in an emergency.

definition: to give permission for something to happen

sentences.text: The rules do not allow food inside.
This ticket allows museum entry.
My schedule does not allow much free time.

definition: to believe that something will happen

sentences.text: We expect the bus to arrive soon.
I did not expect such a quick reply.
The company expects sales to grow.

definition: to find or learn something for the first time

sentences.text: They hope to discover a better solution.
We discovered a small error.
She discovered a new way to study.

definition: to think carefully about something before deciding

sentences.text: You should consider all the possibilities.
We are considering moving abroad.
Please consider my proposal.

definition: to go to an event, meeting, or class

sentences.text: I will attend the conference tomorrow.
Many people attended the event.
She attends an English class twice a week.

definition: to form a picture or idea in your mind

sentences.text: Can you imagine living in another country?
I cannot imagine life without the internet.
She imagined running her own business.

definition: to take the place of something old, broken, or missing

sentences.text: We need to replace the damaged part.
They replaced the old windows.
Can technology replace human judgment?

definition: to stay in the same place or condition

sentences.text: Please remain calm during the test.
Several questions remain unanswered.
The shop will remain closed until Monday.

definition: to act in a particular way

sentences.text: Children should behave politely in public.
The machine is behaving normally.
He behaved calmly during the argument.

definition: to speak or write about something briefly

sentences.text: He did not mention the problem.
She mentioned your name.
I forgot to mention one detail.

definition: to know someone or something because you have seen it before

sentences.text: I did not recognise him at first.
She recognised the song immediately.
It is important to recognise your mistakes.

definition: to give someone confidence or support

sentences.text: Good teachers encourage students to ask questions.
Her friends encouraged her to apply.
The program encourages regular practice.

definition: to answer someone or react to something

sentences.text: Please respond to the email today.
He did not respond to my question.
The system responds quickly.

definition: easy to use or suitable for a particular situation

sentences.text: Online banking is very convenient.
Is Friday convenient for you?
The hotel is in a convenient location.

definition: a difficult task that tests someone's ability

sentences.text: Learning a new language can be a challenge.
The team accepted the challenge.
Finding time is my biggest challenge.

definition: a situation that makes it possible to do something useful

sentences.text: This course is a good opportunity to practise speaking.
She had the opportunity to work abroad.
Do not miss this opportunity.
